---
title: "Analysis for the 'Hidden Attitudes' paper"
author: "Daiki Hiramori and Kairi Shimabukuro"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
    pdf_document:
        latex_engine: xelatex
geometry: margin = 1in
mainfont: Times New Roman
fontsize: 11pt
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{xltxtra}
  - \usepackage{zxjatype}
  - \usepackage[hiragino-pron]{zxjafont}
---

R code for a paper tentatively titled “Hidden Attitudes? Reassessing Support for Same-Sex Marriage in Japan through a List Experiment”

# Data Analysis

```{r}
#penalize excessive significant figures
options(digits = 3)

#prevent scientific notation
options(scipen = 10)

#packages used in this analysis
library(tidyverse)
library(list)
library(tableone)
library(scales)
```

```{r}
#import raw data
data <- read_csv("data/rawdata.csv", locale = locale(encoding = "CP932"),
                 show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#recode variables

#treatment group vs. control group
table(data$cellname)
table(data$cid)

data <- data %>%
  mutate(treat = case_when(
    cid >= 1  & cid <= 10 ~ 1,
    cid >= 11 & cid <= 20 ~ 0))

100*prop.table(table(data$cellname, data$treat), margin = 1)

#direct question
table(data$q10)
data <- data %>%
  mutate(direct = recode(q10,
                         `1` = 1,
                         `2` = 0))

100*prop.table(table(data$q10, data$direct), margin = 1)
```

```{r}
#convert the format
data <- as.data.frame(data)
```

```{r}
#delete satisficers
table(data$q7_5)
100*prop.table(table(data$q7_5))

data <- data %>%
  filter(q7_5 == 2)
```

```{r}
#check covariate balance
vars <- c("q5", "AGE", "q4", "q8")
table1 <- CreateTableOne(
     vars = vars,
     strata = "treat",
     data = data,
     factorVars = c("q5", "q4", "q8"))
print(table1)

#check design effects
ict.test(y = data$q9_1fa, treat = data$treat, J = 4)

#check ceiling and floor effects
table(data$q9_1fa[data$treat == 0])
100*prop.table(table(data$q9_1fa[data$treat == 0]) )

table(data$q9_1fa[data$treat == 1])
100*prop.table(table(data$q9_1fa[data$treat == 1]))
```

```{r}
#calculate list experiment difference in means
diff.in.means.results <- ictreg(q9_1fa ~ 1, data = data, 
                  treat = "treat", J=4, method = "lm")
summary(diff.in.means.results)

#direct question estimate
mean(data$direct, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#fit base models on full sample
fit.list <- ictreg(q9_1fa ~ q5 + AGE + q4 + q8, data = data, 
                    treat = "treat", J = 4, method = "ml")

fit.sens <- glm(direct ~ q5 + AGE + q4 + q8, 
                 data = data %>% filter(treat == 0), 
                 family = binomial("logit"))

#gender subgroups
data <- data %>%
  mutate(gender = case_when(
    q5 == 1 ~ "Men",
    q5 == 2 ~ "Women",
    q5 == 3 ~ "Non-binary"
  ))

for (grp in c("Men", "Women", "Non-binary")) {
  cat(grp)
  print(predict(fit.list, direct.glm = fit.sens,
        newdata = data %>% filter(gender == grp),
        newdata.direct = data %>% filter(treat == 0, gender == grp),
        se.fit = TRUE, avg = TRUE))
}

#age subgroups
data <- data %>%
  mutate(age_group = case_when(
    AGE < 40 ~ "Younger",
    AGE >= 40 & AGE < 60 ~ "Middle",
    AGE >= 60 & AGE < 70 ~ "Older"
  ))

for (grp in c("Younger", "Middle", "Older")) {
  cat(grp)
  print(predict(fit.list, direct.glm = fit.sens,
        newdata = data %>% filter(age_group == grp),
        newdata.direct = data %>% filter(treat == 0, age_group == grp),
        se.fit = TRUE, avg = TRUE))
}

#education subgroups
data <- data %>%
  mutate(edu = case_when(
    q4 <= 2 ~ "HS or less",
    q4 >= 3 ~ "Higher ed"
  ))

for (grp in c("HS or less", "Higher ed")) {
  cat(grp)
  print(predict(fit.list, direct.glm = fit.sens,
        newdata = data %>% filter(edu == grp),
        newdata.direct = data %>% filter(treat == 0, edu == grp),
        se.fit = TRUE, avg = TRUE))
}

#political ideology subgroups
data <- data %>%
  mutate(political = case_when(
    q8 <= 3 ~ "Liberal",
    q8 == 4 ~ "Center",
    q8 >= 5 ~ "Conservative"
  ))

for (grp in c("Liberal", "Center", "Conservative")) {
  cat(grp)
  print(predict(fit.list, direct.glm = fit.sens,
        newdata = data %>% filter(political == grp),
        newdata.direct = data %>% filter(treat == 0, political == grp),
        se.fit = TRUE, avg = TRUE))
}
```

```{r}
#plot the results

#Code for this figure was developed with assistance from Claude (Anthropic); the underlying analysis and all reported estimates are from the authors' own statistical models

library(tidyverse)

# ---- 1. Data extracted from the analysis output -----------------------------

results <- tribble(
  ~category,    ~subgroup,        ~list_est, ~list_se, ~direct_est, ~direct_se,
  "Overall",    "Overall",        0.580,     0.0469,   0.688,       0.0144,
  "Gender",     "Men",            0.505,     0.0610,   0.613,       0.0221,
  "Gender",     "Women",          0.650,     0.0605,   0.758,       0.0190,
  "Gender",     "Non-binary",     0.745,     0.1094,   0.882,       0.0253,
  "Age",        "Younger (20-39)",  0.562,     0.0722,   0.730,       0.0216,
  "Age",        "Middle (40-59)", 0.584,     0.0420,   0.676,       0.0153,
  "Age",        "Older (60-69)",  0.596,     0.0679,   0.643,       0.0252,
  "Education",  "High school or less",     0.647,     0.0658,   0.708,       0.0237,
  "Education",  "Junior college or above",      0.552,     0.0495,   0.679,       0.0168,
  "Ideology",   "Liberal",        0.560,     0.0789,   0.760,       0.0250,
  "Ideology",   "Center",         0.594,     0.0440,   0.717,       0.0162,
  "Ideology",   "Conservative",   0.571,     0.0593,   0.643,       0.0199
)

# ---- 2. Reshape to long format and compute 95% CIs in percentage points ----

plot_data <- results %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(list_est, list_se, direct_est, direct_se),
    names_to = c("measure", ".value"),
    names_pattern = "(list|direct)_(est|se)"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    measure = recode(measure, list = "List experiment", direct = "Direct question"),
    measure = factor(measure, levels = c("List experiment", "Direct question")),
    est = est * 100,
    se  = se * 100,
    ci_low  = est - 1.96 * se,
    ci_high = est + 1.96 * se,
    category = factor(category,
                       levels = c("Overall", "Gender", "Age", "Education", "Ideology")),
    subgroup = fct_inorder(subgroup) %>% fct_rev()
  )

# ---- 3. Plot: vertical dot-and-error-bar plot, percent y-axis --------------

p <- plot_data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = est, y = subgroup, shape = measure)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(xmin = ci_low, xmax = ci_high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
    width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.4, color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
    size = 2.2, fill = "white", color = "black", stroke = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", est)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.5),
    vjust = -1.1, size = 2.7,
    family = "sans",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_grid(category ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y", switch = "y") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("List experiment" = 16, "Direct question" = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 100, 20), "%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Estimated support (%)",
    y = NULL,
    shape = NULL,
    title = "Figure 1. Support for same-sex marriage in Japan: list experiment vs. direct question",
    caption = "Note. Error bars indicate 95% confidence intervals."
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 10, base_family = "sans") +
  theme(
    strip.placement = "outside",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, size = 9, face = "plain"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "grey85", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic", color = "black", hjust = 0)
  )

p

# ---- 4. Save -------------------------------------------------------------------
ggsave("list_vs_direct.png", p, path = "figure", width = 8, height = 9, dpi = 320)
```
